response.justification: The candidate is a strong, versatile senior engineer, but his professional C++ experience is limited to a roughly seven-month stint at National Instruments in 2014-2015, and C++ does not even appear in his current skills list. His career since has centered on web and full-stack work in TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang. While his low-latency DLang work at Sociomantic demonstrates real systems and performance engineering ability, the role demands deep, current C++/STL expertise plus industrial cybersecurity (IEC 62443, SDL) and MQTT/IoT experience, none of which is evidenced. This is a substantial mismatch with the core requirements.
strengths: Proven high-performance, low-latency systems work (async I/O, fibers, caching) handling millions of requests per second
Strong engineering culture: TDD, CI/CD, testing tools authorship, and experience mentoring and running workshops
Exposure to event-driven architectures and distributed/geo-data platforms deployed on Kubernetes
concerns: Professional C++ experience is minimal and dated (~7 months in 2014-2015); C++ absent from his listed skills
No evidence of industrial cybersecurity, IEC 62443, SDL practices, or OT protocols (MQTT, Modbus, OPC UA)
Career trajectory is web/full-stack and product-oriented, not embedded, industrial automation, or C++ systems architecture
